reasoning: The base rate of lethal clashes on the Sino-Indian border is extremely low, with only one such incident (in 2020) over the last 50 years. Recent developments further minimize this risk: in October 2024, both countries reached an agreement to end their military standoff along the Line of Actual Control (LAC). While May 2025 saw a severe India-Pakistan military clash where China rhetorically and materially supported Pakistan, China avoided any direct military confrontation with India, indicating a strong preference to avoid a two-front war or direct escalation. The crowd forecast on May 29 was 0.04; with the passage of another 10 days of no incidents and the May crisis subsiding into a ceasefire, a slight decay to 0.03 is appropriate given the very brief remaining time horizon (less than four months) before the September 30, 2025 resolution date. Events like the Dalai Lama's 90th birthday in July 2025 may cause diplomatic strain, but a shift from the current post-2024 de-escalation posture to a lethal military exchange within this short window remains highly improbable.
